text: I want a quick mental math way to estimate investment doubling time.
Let's build it from one concrete case. If something grows at about 8% per year, the Rule of 72 says 72 divided by 8 gives a rough doubling time. What do you get?
72 divided by 8 is 9, so about 9 years.
Right. I captured that in lesson 01: the rule estimates doubling time, not exact year-by-year value. Now try 6% annual growth: what does 72 / 6 suggest?
At 6%, I think doubling takes about 12 years.
Yes. Lesson 02 now has the 6% example. One more check before we move on: at 9%, what estimate does the shortcut give?
72 divided by 9 is 8, so around 8 years.
Correct. You can now use the shortcut. Next time we should look at why 72 is used instead of a rounder number like 70.
